- G 12px at (35,45) has a p 1px child at (35,45) - this is the start point for the p chain
- G 12px at (48,54) has a p 1px child at (48,54) - this is the end point for the p chain
- p 20px at (41,49) is the p chain's moving object

Looking at the earlier successful p chain clear in level 1/early level 2:

The p 20px at (41,49) is already partway along the path from (35,45) to (48,54). It needs to continue moving toward (48,54). But clicking (41,49) doesn't move it.

Let me try clicking the w segment at (44,51) to push the p 20px toward (48,54). I already tried this but let me check if anything changed.
Actually, I already tried clicking (44,51) and it didn't change anything. Let me try clicking the M center of the G 12px at (48,54), which is the endpoint. The M center would be at (48,54).
Wait, the G 12px at (48,54) has a p 1px child, not an M 1px child. Let me check what the M centers are for the G 12px objects.
Actually, looking at the earlier pattern:
- Each chain has a 20px diamond-shaped object with an M 1px center
- The 20px object moves along a w path toward a 12px endpoint
- When the 20px reaches the end, clicking a w segment near the endpoint clears the chain
For the p chain:
- p 20px at (41,49) with M 1px center
- G 12px at (35,45) with p 1px child (start point)
- G 12px at (48,54) with p 1px child (end point)
- w path: (37,46), (38,47), ..., (44,51), (45,52), (46,53)
The p 20px is at (41,49), which is between (38,47) and (44,51). It needs to move toward (48,54).
Let me try clicking the w segment at (46,53), which is closer to the endpoint (48,54).
